sourceNote: AFM-only training transcription from Viking Air PSM 1-63-1A Revision 53, Section 3.3.4, pages 3-14 to 3-15. The memory list contains only the five actions printed in bold. Crew-role fields are trainer UI assignments only; they are not approved PF/PM callouts or operator SOP. Verify applicability against the aircraft-specific AFM, amendments, supplements, and operator procedures.

memory.action: Set Maximum Power — Advance both power levers to the torque, T5, or NG limit, whichever is reached first; ensure the PROP lever of the operating engine is at MAX RPM (96% NP).
Aircraft control — Adjust pitch attitude to maintain altitude; maintain directional control with rudder.
Power lever of inoperative engine — Retard to the 10 PSI torque position, approximately zero thrust.
PROP lever of inoperative engine — FEATHER.
FUEL lever of inoperative engine — OFF.

flow.action: Trim aircraft as required. Flaps may remain retracted or be extended to 10° if necessary.
Compute and, if necessary, set Maximum Continuous Power.
BOOST PUMP switch for inoperative engine — OFF.
GENERATOR switch of inoperative engine — OFF.
BLEED AIR switch of inoperative engine — OFF.
FUEL OFF emergency shut-off switch of inoperative engine — OFF.
Check generator load on the operative engine; reduce electrical consumption as required to remain at or below 1.0 on the loadmeter.
Use fuel as necessary to remain within center-of-gravity limits for the remainder of the flight.
